skill.name: repo-intake-and-plan
skill.description: Narrow helper skill for README-first AI repo reproduction. Scans repositories, reads README and common project files, extracts documented commands, classifies inference, evaluation, and training candidates, and returns the smallest trustworthy reproduction plan.
skill.summary: Scans AI/ML repositories to extract documented commands and generate minimal reproduction plans.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 29 patterns that are all false positives. Backtick detections in repo-scan-rules.md are markdown documentation formatting around filenames, not shell execution. PowerShell and crypto warnings in Python files are string literals for language detection and configuration, not actual code execution or weak cryptography. The skill performs read-only repository scanning with no network access or command execution capabilities.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer false positives - shell backticks in documentation
low_findings.description: The static scanner detected backtick patterns in repo-scan-rules.md, but these are markdown code formatting around filenames (e.g., README.md), not Ruby/shell backtick execution. This is documentation, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer false positives - weak crypto detection
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm warnings in agents/openai.yaml, SKILL.md, and Python files are false positives. The YAML file contains only metadata configuration. The Python files use standard libraries for non-cryptographic purposes.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer false positives - PowerShell string literal
low_findings.description: The PowerShell detection at extract_commands.py line 186 is a string literal in a tuple used for language identification in regex matching, not actual PowerShell command execution.

content.user_title: Scan repositories and plan AI reproduction
content.value_statement: Manually scanning AI repositories for reproduction commands is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates README-first analysis to extract documented commands and generate minimal trustworthy reproduction plans.
actual_capabilities: Scan repository structure and identify key files like README, requirements, and configuration files
Extract shell commands from README markdown code blocks and inline text
Classify commands into inference, evaluation, training, setup, and asset categories
Generate minimal reproduction plan with risk and ambiguity notes
Identify high-signal directories like configs, scripts, notebooks, and checkpoints
limitations: Does not install environments, download assets, or execute commands
Does not generate final reports or handle end-to-end orchestration
Cannot analyze repositories without README or common project files
Command classification is conservative and may mark ambiguous commands as other

use_cases.title: AI Research Reproduction
use_cases.description: Quickly analyze a new ML repository to understand documented commands and plan the smallest viable reproduction workflow.

use_cases.title: Codebase Onboarding
use_cases.description: Get oriented with a new AI project by extracting all documented commands and understanding the project structure before diving in.

use_cases.title: Repository Triage
use_cases.description: Evaluate multiple repositories to identify which have clear reproduction paths and which lack documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Repository Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan the repository at [PATH], extract all documented commands from the README, and list them by category.

prompt_templates.title: Inference-First Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this repository and identify all inference or demo commands. Prioritize commands that can run without training or large downloads.

prompt_templates.title: Full Reproduction Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan this repository thoroughly. Extract setup commands, asset download instructions, and all training, inference, and evaluation commands. Recommend the minimal path to reproduction with noted risks.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Repository Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan these three repositories and compare their documented commands, noting which has the clearest reproduction path and fewest ambiguities.

output_examples.input: Scan the diffusers repository for inference commands
output: Key commands: python src/diffusers/cli/diffusers.py --help, accelerate launch examples/text_to_image/train_text_to_image.py, python examples/text_to_image/text_to_image.py --prompt
Recommendation: Start with CLI help command for smoke test, then text-to-image example

output_examples.input: Classify commands in a Stable Diffusion README
output: Setup: pip install -r requirements.txt, conda env create -f environment.yml
Training: python train.py --data-dir, accelerate launch train_text_to_image.py

best_practices: Always verify extracted commands against the original README before execution
Prioritize commands explicitly documented in README over those inferred from filenames
Note ambiguities and request clarification when command classification is uncertain
anti_patterns: Do not execute extracted commands without user confirmation and environment verification
Do not assume command classifications are correct when documentation is ambiguous
Do not use this skill for repositories without README or clear documentation

faq.question: What file types does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: The skill scans README.md, README, requirements.txt, environment.yml/yaml, pyproject.toml, setup.py/cfg, Dockerfile, and high-signal directories like configs, scripts, examples, and notebooks.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. This skill only scans and analyzes repository files. It extracts and classifies commands but does not execute them. Execution requires separate user action.

faq.question: How are commands classified?
faq.answer: Commands are classified by section context and keyword matching. Inference includes predict, generate, demo. Evaluation includes eval, benchmark, validate. Training includes train, finetune, pretrain. Ambiguous commands are marked as other.

faq.question: What if the repository has no README?
faq.answer: The skill will flag the absence of README as a warning and report limited findings based on detected files and directories. Reproduction planning is not recommended without documentation.

faq.question: Can this skill download model checkpoints?
faq.answer: No. This skill identifies documented asset download commands but does not execute downloads. It flags asset requirements for the user to handle separately.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for non-Python projects?
faq.answer: The skill is optimized for Python-based ML/AI repositories. It can scan other project types but command classification may be less accurate for non-standard tooling.
